home: Zhang Zeduan, *Along the River During the Qingming Festival* (detail), 12th century
media-studies: Édouard Manet, *A Bar at the Folies-Bergère*, 1882
art-appreciation: Pieter Bruegel the Elder, *The Peasant Wedding*, 1567
representation: Kitagawa Utamaro, *Two Women by a Bamboo Blind*, c. 1797
calendar: Pieter Bruegel the Elder, *The Hunters in the Snow*, 1565
tone-and-form: Peter Paul Rubens, *Head of a Boy (Nicolaas Rubens)*, c. 1619
mark-making: Peter Paul Rubens, *Anatomical Studies*, pen and ink, c. 1605
composition: Clara Peeters, *Still Life with Cheeses, Almonds and Pretzels*, c. 1613
drawn-from-life: Ilya Repin, *Portrait of a Russian Peasant*, 1871
the-great-wave: Gustave Le Gray, *The Great Wave, Sète*, 1857
sea-of-steps: Frederick H. Evans, *A Sea of Steps, Wells Cathedral*, 1903
st-pauls-spires: Alvin Langdon Coburn, *St. Paul's and Other Spires*, c. 1909
canon-de-chelle: Timothy O'Sullivan, *Ancient Ruins in the Cañon de Chelle*, 1873
lincoln-cathedral: Frederick H. Evans, *Lincoln Cathedral From the Castle*, 1898
girl-with-washington: Southworth & Hawes, *Girl with Portrait of George Washington*, c. 1850
cloud-sequence: Alfred Stieglitz, *Music: A Sequence of Ten Cloud Photographs, No. 1*, 1922
articles-of-glass: William Henry Fox Talbot, *Articles of Glass*, 1844
yosemite-mosquito-camp: Eadweard Muybridge, *Valley of the Yosemite, from Mosquito Camp*, 1872
yosemite-rocky-ford: Eadweard Muybridge, *Valley of the Yosemite, from Rocky Ford*, 1872
lissitzky-schwitters: El Lissitzky, *Kurt Schwitters*, c. 1924 — a photomontage portrait of the great collagist
rubens-title-page: Peter Paul Rubens, title-page design for *Obsidio Bredana*, 1626
impossible-bouquet: Ambrosius Bosschaert the Elder, *A Still Life of Flowers*, 1609–10 — flowers that never bloom together, composed anyway
gsell-advertisement: Émile Gsell, photographic advertisement, 1860s — one name card commanding a hundred photographs
kawase-temple: Hasui Kawase, *Zôjô-ji Temple in Shiba*, 1925 — a print designed to circulate
album-leaf: Aoki Shukuya, *Double Album of Landscape Studies after Ikeno Taiga*, 18th century
seventh-regiment: Underwood & Underwood, *Mother, Wife, and Sweetheart Watching Boys of the Seventh Regiment as They Marched Away to War*, c. 1917 — the caption tells you who they are and how to feel
rebel-sharpshooter: Alexander Gardner, *Home of a Rebel Sharpshooter*, 1863 — the soldier's body was moved and posed for the composition
migrant-mother: Dorothea Lange, *Migrant Mother*, 1936 — one frame chosen from six exposures, then cropped
skull-cigarette: Vincent van Gogh, *Skull of a Skeleton with Burning Cigarette*, 1885 — what it shows is simple; what it suggests is the lesson
mummy-portrait: Mummy portrait of a man wearing an ivy wreath, Roman Egypt, c. 101 CE — wreath, gold, gaze: codes at work for 1,900 years
calling-of-matthew: Caravaggio, *The Calling of Saint Matthew*, 1599 — one light source, five gestures: a room you can read
nadar-taylor: Nadar, *Baron Isidore Taylor*, c. 1865 — an icon: it means by resembling
talbot-lace-index: William Henry Fox Talbot, *Lace*, c. 1844 — an index: made by direct contact with the thing itself
deer-mandala: *Deer Mandala of the Kasuga Shrine*, 15th century — a symbol: it means by convention alone
vanitas-schaak: B. Schaak, *Vanitas Still Life*, 1675–1700 — skull, lantern, hourglass, book: the genre's repertoire
vanitas-schoor: Aelbert van der Schoor, *Vanitas Still Life*, c. 1660 — the same codes, a different picture: repetition and difference
at-the-telephone: Aleksandr Rodchenko, *At the Telephone*, 1928 — name the camera position; then name what it does to you
banqueting-sketch: Peter Paul Rubens, sketch for the Banqueting House ceiling, c. 1630 — a plan another hand could paint from
peasant-wedding: Pieter Bruegel the Elder, *The Peasant Wedding*, 1567 — every figure a function: bride, piper, steward, pourer
whittier-classroom: Frances Benjamin Johnston, *English Literature — Lesson on Whittier*, Hampton Institute, 1899
hampton-geography: Frances Benjamin Johnston, *Geography — Studying the Seasons*, Hampton Institute, 1899 — work checked together, in the room
vivarini-exorcism: Antonio Vivarini, *Saint Peter Martyr Exorcizing a Woman Possessed by a Devil*, c. 1450 — hero, villain, victim, helpers: Propp's functions, five centuries early
wanderer: Caspar David Friedrich, *Wanderer Above the Sea of Fog*, 1818 — who is he? what does he see? the enigma code at work
kasebier-wild-west: Gertrude Käsebier, *American Indian Portrait*, c. 1899 — a studio construction: who made the choices in this image, and for whom?
lincoln-mcclellan: Alexander Gardner, *Lincoln and McClellan at Antietam*, 1862 — the photo-op is older than the word
folies-bergere: Édouard Manet, *A Bar at the Folies-Bergère*, 1882 — who is looking at whom? the mirror refuses to agree
cahun-gaze: Claude Cahun, *Self-Portrait*, 1920 — a look sent back: the gaze refused
rivera-self-portrait: Diego Rivera, *Self-Portrait*, 1907 — the maker examining himself
good-glass-of-beer: Édouard Manet, *A Good Glass of Beer*, 1873 — gratification, personified
above-fifth-avenue: Underwood & Underwood, *Above Fifth Avenue, Looking North*, c. 1905 — the industry at work: somebody pays for this vantage
emperors-table: Gustave Le Gray, *Preparation of the Emperor's Table, Camp de Châlons*, 1857 — photographed on imperial commission: the frame belonged to the payer
lindbergh-1927: Underwood & Underwood, *Charles Lindbergh*, 1927 — the first global media event: one man, every channel
moon-atlas: Loewy & Puiseux, plate from the *Atlas Photographique de la Lune*, c. 1900 — the whole surface, plate by plate, so no region goes unexamined
the-magpie: Claude Monet, *The Magpie*, 1869 — winter light, and the quiet after
